# Katia Hollaar – Portfolio

> Full-stack web developer integrating AI into real products and capturing visual stories through photography.

This site showcases my work as a full-stack engineer (React / Next.js / TypeScript) and my photography, with a strong focus on AI integration, performance, accessibility, and modern UX.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://afkatja.github.io/): Overview of my work in web development, AI-integrated projects, and photography.
- [Projects](https://afkatja.github.io/web): Selected web applications using React and Next.js, including:
  - AI-powered travel and trading assistants using LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
  - Interfaces that integrate third-party APIs, authentication, and real-time data.
- [Photography](https://afkatja.github.io/photography): Curated photography series and visual storytelling.
- [About](https://afkatja.github.io/about): Background, skills, AI experience, and how I work both independently and in teams.
- [Contact](https://afkatja.github.io/contact): Contact details and ways to collaborate.

## LLMs, RAG & AI Integration

I design and build full-stack AI features that feel native to the product, not bolted on. On the frontend, I work with React and Next.js to create responsive, performant interfaces for prompts, conversations, feedback, and error states. On the backend, I use Node.js and serverless/edge runtimes to orchestrate model calls, tools, and data pipelines.
### LLM-powered web applications

I integrate large language models into production-like web apps using both commercial APIs and open-source models, connected via HTTP/SDK. Typical projects include travel planners, research assistants, and task-focused agents that combine conversational UX with real data and business logic.

### Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)

I build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workflows that ground model outputs in external data sources. This includes structuring and indexing content, wiring retrieval into prompts, and tuning responses for relevance and clarity so users can trust what they see.

### Agents, SLMs and open-source models

Beyond single-call prompts, I work with AI agents that use tools, call functions, and execute multi-step flows to accomplish user goals. I experiment with small language models (SLMs) and open-source models to balance latency, cost, privacy, and control, choosing the right model family for each use case.

### Generation and evaluation pipelines

I set up generation and evaluation pipelines that make AI features easier to iterate on. This includes prompt design, regression-style test sets, qualitative and quantitative evaluations, and feedback loops that capture real user behavior, so changes can be tested instead of guessed.

### Developer experience and AI tooling

I care deeply about developer experience when building with AI. I configure AI-assisted development environments, including editor setups and tools like Windsurf, to speed up iteration on prompts, agents, and RAG flows while keeping the architecture understandable for the rest of the team.

## Technical notes

- Tech stack: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Node.js, Supabase, potstgres, Sanity.io, Netlify/Vercel, GitHub CI/CD and Actions
- Focus areas: AI feature integration, frontend performance, accessibility, responsive design, and DX
- Primary audience: teams looking for a full-stack developer who can both build great interfaces and integrate AI capabilities into web products
